utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , cheryl . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing well . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing well . so i know the nurse told you a little bit about dax . i'd like to tell dax about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: cheryl is a 34-year-old female with a past medical history significant for hypertension , who presents today with back pain . cheryl , what happened to your back ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i've been walking a lot lately . i've been walking to ... 30 minutes to an hour or so a day . and all of a sudden , um , when i was walking , my , um , back just kind of seized up on me . and i do n't really know what it was . maybe i was going a little bit faster . but it just all kind of clenched .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so you felt like , maybe like a spasm or something like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how many days ago was that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that was about six days ago now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and what have you taken for the pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been taking ibuprofen . um , and then i've been putting some heat on it . but it's still pretty stiff .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , and did you have any trauma before that happened ? were you doing anything strenuous like crossfit or lifting boxes or anything like that before you went for , for the walk ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have been lifting more , um , probably around three times a week . so i do n't know if it was because i was doing deadlifts that day and then walked .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , maybe i was using my back more than my legs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and was it any particular area in your back ? was it the lower back ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it was .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . on one side versus the other ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , kind of both equally .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and any numbing or tingling in your legs or your feet ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i have n't felt anything like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . any weakness in your lower extremities ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and then in terms of your blood pressure , how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i got that cuff that you suggested the ... our ... the last visit , and i've been doing readings at home . and that's been looking great , too . i've been watching my diet . again , my boyfriend's been great and dieting with me so i do n't have to do it alone . and everything's been good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . excellent . and you're taking the lisinopril ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . wonderful . okay . so i know you did a review of systems sheet with the nurse , and i know you endorse , you know , this back pain . um , do you have any other symptoms ? fever , chills , congestion , cough , chest pain , shortness of breath ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have a little bit of nasal congestion , but that's just from my seasonal allergies .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , let's go ahead . i want to do a quick physical exam on you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ? hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . so good- you know , here in the office , your vital signs look great . your blood pressure's really well controlled , which is good . so that's a good job . so i'm going to take a listen to your heart and lungs . i'm going to examine your back , and i'm going to let you know what i find . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . so on physical examination , you know , everything looks good . you know , on your heart exam , i do hear that slight two out of six systolic ejection murmur , but you've had that before . that seems stable to me . on your back exam , you do have some pain to palpation on the right lateral aspect of your lumbar spine , and you do have pain with flexion and extension as well , and you have a negative straight leg raise . so what does that mean ? so we're going to go over that . okay ? let's ... let me look at some of your results , though , first . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we did an x-ray before you saw me , so let's look at that . hey , dragon , show me the back x-ray . so looking here at this x-ray of the lumbar spine , everything looks good . there's good boney alignment . there's no obvious fracture , you know , which is not surprising based on your history . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the labs . and your labs that we did before you came in all look great . there's no elevated white blood cell count . there's no signs of infection . again , those are all really good . okay ? so let me go over with you about my assessment and my plan for you . so for your first problem , this back pain , i think you have a lumbar strain , and , you know , that might've happened , you know , lifting something or exercising . and so what i want to do is prescribe meloxicam , 15 milligrams once a day . uh , i want you ... you can ice the area , and you can also apply heat sometimes as well . um , you know , i'm going to refer you to physical therapy just to do some strengthening exercises of your back , um , because i do want you to continue to be able to work out and exercise . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and for your last problem , your high blood pressure , again , everything looks great here . um , you know , i think you're doing a really good job with that as well . i want you to continue on the lisinopril , 10 milligrams a day . and then , uh , let me know if you notice any increases in your blood pressure readings . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you need a refill of the lisinopril ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i do , actually .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon ? order lisinopril 10 milligrams po daily . okay . uh , so the nurse will be in soon , and she'll get you checked out . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . hey , dragon ? finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 34-year-old female with a history of hypertension presenting with back pain.
Back pain
The doctor asks the patient to describe the onset of her back pain.
The patient reports walking 30 to 60 minutes daily and experienced a sudden back spasm while walking.
The doctor asks if the patient experienced a muscle spasm.
The doctor asks for the duration of the symptoms.
The back pain began six days ago.
The doctor asks what the patient has taken for the pain.
The patient has been using ibuprofen and heat, but the back remains stiff.
The doctor asks if there was any trauma or strenuous activity prior to the onset of pain.
The patient lifts weights three times a week and performed deadlifts on the day the pain started.
The patient suspects she may have used her back more than her legs during deadlifts.
The doctor asks for the location of the back pain.
The pain is located in the lower back.
The doctor asks if the pain is unilateral or bilateral.
The pain is bilateral.
The doctor asks about numbness or tingling in the legs or feet.
The patient denies numbness or tingling.
The doctor asks about lower extremity weakness.
The patient denies lower extremity weakness.
Hypertension
The doctor asks about the status of the patient's blood pressure.
The patient reports good home blood pressure readings and dietary modifications.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking lisinopril.
The patient confirms she is taking lisinopril.
The doctor asks about other symptoms including fever, chills, congestion, cough, chest pain, or shortness of breath.
The patient reports nasal congestion due to seasonal allergies.
The doctor notes the patient's blood pressure is well-controlled and declares intent to perform a physical exam.
Physical exam findings include a stable 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, pain to palpation on the right lateral lumbar spine, pain with flexion and extension, and a negative straight leg raise.
Lumbar spine x-ray shows good bony alignment with no fractures.
Labs are within normal limits with no signs of infection.
The doctor assesses the patient with a lumbar strain.
The doctor prescribes meloxicam 15 mg once daily.
The doctor recommends ice and heat application and refers the patient to physical therapy.
The doctor instructs the patient to continue lisinopril 10 mg daily.
The doctor asks the patient to report any elevated blood pressure readings.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill of lisinopril.
The patient confirms she needs a refill.
The doctor orders a refill of lisinopril 10 mg daily.
